title: [memsafety] undefined variable in lichen_announce_parse
description:
Reviewed last 20 commits (mostly worker merges fixing RPL trickle, routing/announce, hal, coap_dtn, l2, replay). Focused on C files in lichen/subsys/lichen/. 

No strcpy/strcat/sprintf found. All memcpy/snprintf use explicit sizes or sizeof()/passed bounds (e.g. announce.c:116 buf_len check before memcpy, coap_*.c use sizeof(id_str)). Saturating arithmetic in trickle.c prevents int overflow. 

However, in lichen/subsys/lichen/routing/announce.c:243 (touched in project-LICHEN-3h2u/vu0r fixes): `announce->flags = rx_ch;` where `rx_ch` is undeclared (rx_channel set on next line from data[1]). This is undefined behavior / compile error. Recent changes added many bounds checks and SECURITY comments but introduced this.

See also parse bounds (lines 227-241 good) and lock-protected snapshots in build_announce_frame to prevent TOCTOU buffer overflows.
close_reason: Fixed undefined rx_ch variable by setting announce->flags = data[1] to match rx_channel and frame format per CCP-9 and tests. Verified change in parse function. Commit 1e0dd544e pushed.
